description: Built-in providers Amaco detects and auto-configures. Each ships a preset (the non-interactive invocation) and a login command to run outside Amaco when the provider isn't authenticated. Verify any provider with `amaco provider test <id>`.

providers.label: Claude Code
providers.loginNote: Run `claude` once and complete sign-in, or set ANTHROPIC_API_KEY.
notes: Default args: -p with prompt on stdin.
Amaco will configure Claude Code automatically.
providers.installHint: Install Claude Code: `npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code`, then run `claude` once to sign in.

providers.label: Codex CLI
notes: Preset: `codex exec -q` with the prompt on stdin.
Verify with `amaco provider test codex`; log in with `codex login` if prompted.
providers.installHint: Install the Codex CLI: `npm install -g @openai/codex`, then run `codex login`.

providers.label: Gemini CLI
providers.loginNote: Run `gemini` once and sign in with Google, or set GEMINI_API_KEY.
notes: Preset: prompt piped to `gemini` on stdin.
Verify with `amaco provider test gemini`; sign in by running `gemini` once (or set GEMINI_API_KEY).
providers.installHint: Install the Gemini CLI: `npm install -g @google/gemini-cli`, then run `gemini` once to authenticate.

providers.label: OpenCode
notes: Preset: `opencode run` with the prompt as an argument.
Verify with `amaco provider test opencode`; log in with `opencode auth login`.

providers.label: Aider
providers.loginNote: Aider authenticates via API keys — set OPENAI_API_KEY or ANTHROPIC_API_KEY in your environment.
notes: Preset: `aider --message` (one-shot, no auto-commits).
Set OPENAI_API_KEY or ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, then verify with `amaco provider test aider`.
providers.installHint: Install Aider: `python -m pip install aider-install && aider-install`, then set OPENAI_API_KEY or ANTHROPIC_API_KEY.

providers.label: Ollama
providers.loginNote: No login needed (runs locally). Pull the model first: `ollama pull qwen3.5`.
notes: Preset: `ollama run qwen3.5` with the prompt on stdin.
Pull the model first (`ollama pull qwen3.5`), or edit providers.ollama.args for another local model.
No login needed (runs locally). Verify with `amaco provider test ollama`.
providers.installHint: Install Ollama: `curl -fsSL https://ollama.com/install.sh | sh` (Linux/macOS) or download it from https://ollama.com/download.

providers.label: Qwen Code
providers.loginNote: Run `qwen` once and authenticate (or set the API key it prompts for).
notes: Preset: prompt piped to `qwen` on stdin.
Verify with `amaco provider test qwen`; authenticate by running `qwen` once.

providers.label: Crush
providers.loginNote: Crush uses your model provider's API key — set the relevant one (e.g. ANTHROPIC_API_KEY / OPENAI_API_KEY).
notes: Preset: `crush run` with the prompt as an argument.
Set your model provider's API key, then verify with `amaco provider test crush`.
providers.installHint: Install Crush: `brew install charmbracelet/tap/crush` (macOS) or see https://github.com/charmbracelet/crush.

providers.label: Goose
providers.loginNote: Run `goose configure` to set your provider and API key.
notes: Preset: `goose run -t` with the prompt as an argument.
Run `goose configure` to set your provider + key, then verify with `amaco provider test goose`.

providers.label: Cursor CLI
notes: Preset: `cursor-agent -p` with the prompt as an argument.
Log in with `cursor-agent login`, then verify with `amaco provider test cursor`.
providers.installHint: Install the Cursor CLI: `curl https://cursor.com/install -fsS | bash`.

providers.label: Amp
notes: Preset: `amp -x` with the prompt as an argument.
Log in with `amp login`, then verify with `amaco provider test amp`.
